title: Find Duplicate Page Titles
hook: Duplicate titles make a static site harder to scan in search results and browser tabs.
problem: You need to find repeated HTML title text across a built static site.
command: grep -Rho --include='*.html' '<title>[^<]*</title>' public | sed 's#<title>##;s#</title>##' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr
starting_state: A static site has been built into a public directory.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command reads HTML files, extracts title text, and counts repeats.
when_to_use: Use before publishing a static site or after template changes that may duplicate page titles.
when_not_to_use: Do not treat it as a complete SEO audit; it only checks the visible title element.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: A count-sorted list of title strings, with duplicates showing counts greater than one.

commands: grep -Rho --include='*.html' '<title>[^<]*</title>' public | sed 's#<title>##;s#</title>##' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr

shorts.title: Find duplicate page titles.
shorts.voiceover: When every page inherits the same title, search snippets get muddy. Count titles from the built HTML before shipping.
shorts.caption: Count titles in the built site.

linkedin.question: Do you catch duplicate titles from templates before deploy?
linkedin.cta: Audit generated HTML, not just source templates.

youtube.description: Use grep, sed, sort, and uniq to find duplicate title tags in a static site build.

experiments.variant_b: Your template copied one title everywhere.

title: Find Pages Missing Canonical Links
hook: Canonical tags are easy to drop when templates branch.
problem: You need to list generated HTML pages that do not include a canonical link.
command: find public -name '*.html' -print | while read -r f; do grep -qi 'rel="canonical"' "$f" || echo "$f"; done
starting_state: A static site build contains HTML pages, some with canonical tags and some without them.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command scans each HTML file and prints files missing canonical markup.
when_to_use: Use after layout edits, generator upgrades, or route additions.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it to validate whether canonical URLs are correct; it only checks presence.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: A list of HTML files that lack rel="canonical".

commands: find public -name '*.html' -print | while read -r f; do grep -qi 'rel="canonical"' "$f" || echo "$f"; done

shorts.title: Find missing canonicals.
shorts.voiceover: Scan generated pages, not assumptions. This loop prints every HTML file that missed the canonical tag.
shorts.caption: Canonical presence check.

linkedin.question: Have you had one template path silently drop canonical tags?
linkedin.cta: Check the built files after template changes.

youtube.description: Find static HTML pages missing canonical links with find and grep.

title: Find Pages Marked noindex
hook: A leftover noindex can hide a page after launch.
problem: You need to identify generated HTML pages that contain noindex directives.
starting_state: A static site build may contain draft or staging pages with noindex metadata.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command searches generated HTML for noindex.
when_to_use: Use before launch, after moving draft pages, or when a page is not appearing in search.
when_not_to_use: Do not assume every noindex is wrong; some pages should intentionally stay out of search.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Matching file paths and lines containing noindex.

shorts.title: Catch leftover noindex.
shorts.voiceover: Before launch, search the built HTML for noindex so draft metadata does not hide a real page.
shorts.caption: Search generated HTML for noindex.

linkedin.question: Do you audit noindex before publishing a static site?
linkedin.cta: Look for directives in the output directory.

youtube.description: Find noindex directives in generated static HTML.

title: Find Pages Missing Meta Descriptions
hook: Missing descriptions are usually a content template problem, not a mystery.
problem: You need to list HTML pages without a meta description.
command: find public -name '*.html' -print | while read -r f; do grep -qi 'name="description"' "$f" || echo "$f"; done
starting_state: A built static site contains a mix of landing pages, posts, and draft pages.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command reads each HTML file and prints pages missing description metadata.
when_to_use: Use after adding new templates or when checking generated content metadata.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it to judge description quality; it only checks whether the tag exists.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: A list of HTML files missing name="description".

commands: find public -name '*.html' -print | while read -r f; do grep -qi 'name="description"' "$f" || echo "$f"; done

shorts.title: Find pages without descriptions.
shorts.voiceover: A simple loop over generated HTML shows which pages skipped the meta description tag.
shorts.caption: Presence check first, quality pass later.

linkedin.question: Do your generated posts all get descriptions, or only the main pages?
linkedin.cta: Scan output HTML to catch template gaps.

youtube.description: Find static HTML pages missing meta description tags.

title: List URLs from a Sitemap
hook: Before comparing sitemap coverage, print the URLs plainly.
problem: You need to inspect the loc entries inside a sitemap without opening an XML viewer.
starting_state: A static site has generated a sitemap.xml file.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command extracts URL text from sitemap loc elements.
when_to_use: Use when checking sitemap contents during static-site deploys.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it as an XML validator; it is a quick inspection command.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.

shorts.title: Print sitemap URLs.
shorts.voiceover: For a quick sitemap sanity check, extract loc values and read the URLs line by line.
shorts.caption: Turn sitemap XML into a URL list.

linkedin.question: Do you inspect sitemap output after changing routes?
linkedin.cta: Start with a plain URL list from sitemap.xml.

youtube.description: Extract URLs from sitemap.xml with grep and sed.

title: Check robots.txt for a Sitemap Line
hook: A sitemap can exist and still be hard to discover.
problem: You need to confirm robots.txt advertises the sitemap URL.
starting_state: A static site has a robots.txt file and a generated sitemap.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command searches robots.txt for a Sitemap directive.
when_to_use: Use after changing sitemap paths, domains, or static host routing.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it to validate every robots rule; it only checks the sitemap directive.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: The line number and Sitemap directive from robots.txt.

shorts.title: Does robots advertise sitemap?
shorts.voiceover: Check robots.txt for the Sitemap line so crawlers can discover the sitemap directly.
shorts.caption: robots.txt should point at sitemap.xml.

linkedin.question: Do you check robots.txt after moving a static site between domains?
linkedin.cta: Verify the Sitemap directive in the generated file.

youtube.description: Use grep to confirm robots.txt includes a Sitemap directive.

experiments.variant_b: Sitemap exists but is not advertised.

title: Find HTML Pages Missing from the Sitemap
hook: A page can exist in the build but never make it into the sitemap.
problem: You need to compare generated HTML files against sitemap URLs.
command: find public -name '*.html' -print | sed 's#^public#https://example.com#' | while read -r url; do grep -q "$url" public/sitemap.xml || echo "$url"; done
starting_state: A static site build contains HTML files and a sitemap.xml file.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command maps file paths to URLs and prints pages absent from the sitemap.
when_to_use: Use when new routes are not appearing in sitemap output.
when_not_to_use: Do not treat every result as an error; drafts and private pages may be intentionally omitted.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Generated page URLs that do not appear in sitemap.xml.

commands: find public -name '*.html' -print | sed 's#^public#https://example.com#' | while read -r url; do grep -q "$url" public/sitemap.xml || echo "$url"; done

shorts.title: Which pages missed the sitemap?
shorts.voiceover: Map generated HTML paths to public URLs, then print any URL missing from sitemap.xml.
shorts.caption: Compare built pages to sitemap URLs.

linkedin.question: Do you compare generated pages against sitemap output?
linkedin.cta: Catch pages that exist but are not advertised.

youtube.description: Find static HTML pages that are missing from sitemap.xml.

title: Find Broken Internal Links in Built HTML
hook: A broken internal link is easiest to catch before it becomes a 404.
problem: You need to list internal href paths that do not exist in the static build.
command: grep -Rho --include='*.html' 'href="/[^"]*"' public | sed 's#href="##;s#"##' | while read -r path; do test -e "public${path}" || echo "$path"; done | sort -u
starting_state: A static site build contains HTML pages with root-relative links.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command extracts internal links and checks whether the target path exists locally.
when_to_use: Use before deploys, after URL changes, or after moving content between sections.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it for JavaScript-routed apps or remote URLs without adapting the path logic.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Root-relative paths linked from HTML that do not exist in the public directory.

commands: grep -Rho --include='*.html' 'href="/[^"]*"' public | sed 's#href="##;s#"##' | while read -r path; do test -e "public${path}" || echo "$path"; done | sort -u

shorts.title: Find broken internal links.
shorts.voiceover: Extract root-relative hrefs from generated HTML and test whether each target exists in the build.
shorts.caption: Catch broken links before deploy.

linkedin.question: Do you check broken internal links from the built output or source files?
linkedin.cta: Audit the exact HTML you are about to serve.

youtube.description: Find broken root-relative internal links in a static site build.

title: Find Pages Missing og:title
hook: Social previews often fail because one template missed Open Graph tags.
problem: You need to list generated HTML pages without og:title metadata.
command: find public -name '*.html' -print | while read -r f; do grep -qi 'property="og:title"' "$f" || echo "$f"; done
starting_state: A static site build contains pages intended to be shared on social platforms.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command checks each HTML file for og:title metadata.
when_to_use: Use before launch, after changing social preview templates, or when link previews look wrong.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it to validate image sizes or preview rendering; it only checks og:title presence.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: A list of HTML files missing property="og:title".

commands: find public -name '*.html' -print | while read -r f; do grep -qi 'property="og:title"' "$f" || echo "$f"; done

shorts.title: Find missing social titles.
shorts.voiceover: Scan built HTML for og:title so shared links do not fall back to weak defaults.
shorts.caption: Check Open Graph metadata in output.

linkedin.question: Do you audit Open Graph tags before sharing a new page?
linkedin.cta: Check generated HTML, then preview the URL.

youtube.description: Find static HTML pages missing og:title metadata.

title: Find Feed Links Missing from the Sitemap
hook: Your feed can advertise URLs that the sitemap never lists.
problem: You need to compare feed item links against sitemap entries.
command: grep -o '<link>https://example.com/[^<]*</link>' public/feed.xml | sed 's#<link>##;s#</link>##' | while read -r url; do grep -q "$url" public/sitemap.xml || echo "$url"; done
starting_state: A static site build contains both feed.xml and sitemap.xml.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command extracts feed links and prints any URL absent from the sitemap.
when_to_use: Use after changing feed generation, permalink formats, or sitemap filters.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it for feeds with multiple domains without adapting the URL filter.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Feed item URLs that do not appear in sitemap.xml.

commands: grep -o '<link>https://example.com/[^<]*</link>' public/feed.xml | sed 's#<link>##;s#</link>##' | while read -r url; do grep -q "$url" public/sitemap.xml || echo "$url"; done

shorts.title: Compare feed to sitemap.
shorts.voiceover: Extract feed URLs and print the ones missing from sitemap.xml. It catches stale or filtered content fast.
shorts.caption: Feed links should line up with sitemap rules.

linkedin.question: Have you compared feed output against sitemap output after permalink changes?

youtube.description: Compare feed item links against sitemap URLs from static build output.
